message: Close brenner_bot-n5hy: improve CLI test output capture

@@ -519,7 +519,7 @@ {"id":"brenner_bot-mhi","title":"TanStack Store: Create Compare View State Persistence","description":"# Goal Persist the distillation comparison view state so users don't lose their selections. ## Background The CompareView component has UI state that should persist: - leftModel: Which model is in left pane (opus/gpt/gemini) - rightModel: Which model is in right pane - syncScroll: Whether panes scroll together - Per-model scroll positions Currently, refreshing the page resets all this state. ## Store Schema ```typescript interface CompareStore { leftModel: 'opus' | 'gpt' | 'gemini'; rightModel: 'opus' | 'gpt' | 'gemini'; syncScroll: boolean; scrollPositions: Record; // model -> scrollY } ``` ## Implementation ```typescript import { Store } from '@tanstack/store'; const compareStore = new Store({ leftModel: 'opus', rightModel: 'gpt', syncScroll: true, scrollPositions: {}, }); // Persist to localStorage compareStore.subscribe(() => { localStorage.setItem('brenner-compare', JSON.stringify(compareStore.state)); }); // React hook export function useCompareState() { return useStore(compareStore, (s) => s); } export function setLeftModel(model: Model) { compareStore.setState((s) => ({ ...s, leftModel: model })); } ``` ## Integration ```typescript function CompareView() { const { leftModel, rightModel, syncScroll } = useCompareState(); return (
{/* ... */}
); } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Model selections persist - [ ] Sync scroll preference persists - [ ] Per-model scroll positions persist - [ ] State restores on page load ## Files to Create - apps/web/src/stores/compareStore.ts - apps/web/src/hooks/useCompareState.ts ## Files to Modify - apps/web/src/components/distillation/CompareView.tsx","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:20:13.355992416-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:44:46.446051904-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:44:46.446051904-05:00","close_reason":"Obsolete: Compare view feature was removed in commit 34a5aa6","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-mhi","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-59l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:20:36.726561983-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-mqg7","title":"Feature: Operator Intervention Audit Trail","description":"# Goal Implement a formal audit trail for human operator interventions during Brenner Loop sessions, enabling reproducibility, trust verification, and learning from overrides. ## The Gap This Fills Other agents covered: - Human-in-the-loop gates (general concept) - Cockpit runner (orchestration) - Web UI for sessions **What nobody covered:** When a human operator overrides an agent decision (e.g., manually killing a hypothesis, forcing a test to be included, editing an artifact before publish), there is no formal record of: - WHAT was changed - WHO made the change - WHY they made it (rationale) - WHEN it happened relative to agent activities This matters because: 1. **Reproducibility** - Can someone else replicate this research if they dont know a human secretly tweaked the artifact? 2. **Trust** - Was this conclusion reached by agents following the protocol, or did a human push it through? 3. **Learning** - If operators frequently override a certain agent/role, maybe the role prompt needs improvement. 4. **Debugging** - When a session goes wrong, was it agent error or operator error? ## Why This Is Different from Deltas Agent contributions are recorded as DELTA messages with `from: AgentName`. But operator interventions can happen in several ways that bypass the delta flow: 1. **Direct artifact editing** before publish (editing the markdown) 2. **Selective delta exclusion** (compiling without some deltas) 3. **Manual message injection** (posting a DELTA on behalf of an agent) 4. **Override decisions** ("ignore this critique", "force-include this test") 5. **Session control actions** (early termination, forced re-run, role reassignment) ## Proposed Solution ### 1. Operator Intervention Record ```typescript interface OperatorIntervention { id: string; session_id: string; timestamp: string; operator_id: string; // From BRENNER_AGENT_NAME or "human" type: | "artifact_edit" // Direct edit to compiled artifact | "delta_exclusion" // Excluded a delta from compilation | "delta_injection" // Added a delta not from an agent | "decision_override" // Overrode a protocol decision | "session_control" // Terminated, forked, or reset session | "role_reassignment"; // Changed agent-role mappings mid-session target: { message_id?: number; // For delta exclusion/injection artifact_version?: number; item_id?: string; // Hypothesis, test, etc. }; before?: string; // State before intervention (for diffs) after?: string; // State after intervention rationale: string; // Required: WHY did the operator do this? reversible: boolean; reversed_at?: string; reversed_by?: string; } ``` ### 2. Intervention Capture Points | Action | Capture Point | Implementation | |--------|---------------|----------------| | Edit artifact before publish | Compare pre/post in publish action | API route adds intervention record | | Exclude delta | Compile action with exclusion list | Track excluded message_ids | | Manual delta | DELTA message where from = operator | Check if sender matches known agent roster | | Early termination | Session close action | Add "session_control" record | | Role reassignment | Roster change mid-session | Compare before/after roster state | ### 3. Storage and Access **Storage:** Interventions stored as part of session metadata in Agent Mail thread (as INFO: messages with structured payload) or in a separate interventions log. **Access:** - `brenner session interventions ` - List all interventions - Session detail page shows intervention count and expandable list - Compiled artifacts include intervention summary in metadata ### 4. Intervention Severity Levels ``` MINOR: Typo fixes, formatting adjustments MODERATE: Delta exclusion, small edits MAJOR: Killing hypotheses, adding tests, role changes CRITICAL: Session termination, protocol bypass ``` Major/Critical interventions trigger warnings in the UI. ### 5. Reproducibility Mode A "strict" compilation mode that: - Fails if any MAJOR+ interventions exist - Produces a "clean" artifact that is purely agent-derived - Useful for comparing "what would agents have produced alone?" ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `OperatorIntervention` type defined in session types - [ ] Publish action detects and records artifact edits - [ ] Compile action accepts `excludeDeltas` param and records exclusions - [ ] Manual delta detection (sender not in agent roster) - [ ] CLI `brenner session interventions ` command - [ ] Web session detail shows intervention badge/list - [ ] All interventions require `rationale` field - [ ] Compiled artifact metadata includes `intervention_count` ## Dependencies - Agent roster (brenner_bot-5so.12.1.x) - needed to detect "operator vs agent" - Session compile/publish actions (already exist) ## Non-Goals - Not preventing interventions (operators should have full control) - Not implementing approval workflows (thats for future governance) - Not tracking every click/action (only meaningful interventions) ## Background: Why This Matters for Science Real science has lab notebooks that record everything, including mistakes and corrections. The audit trail is the digital equivalent. Without it, a "research result" from a Brenner Loop session cannot be trusted or replicated by others. The Brenner Method emphasizes transparency and honesty about what was done and why. Occams Broom (hiding inconvenient facts) is an anti-pattern. The audit trail ensures operator interventions are visible, not swept under the rug.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"GoldFalcon","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:01:31.405372314-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-01T20:48:36.816169242-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-01T20:48:36.816169242-05:00","close_reason":"Schema, storage layer, and artifact metadata complete. Remaining integration work (publish/compile capture, CLI commands) can be follow-up tasks.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-mqg7","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:38:22.90910216-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-nihv","title":"Feature: Brenner Method Scorecard","description":"# Feature: Brenner Method Scorecard (COMPREHENSIVE REWRITE) ## Purpose Implement the evaluation rubric as automated tooling. Provides per-session and per-contribution scores on Brenner method adherence with actionable feedback. ## CRITICAL REWRITE The original 7-dimension design was **too simplified**. The evaluation rubric has **14 specific criteria** organized by role. The scorecard must align with the actual rubric. ## Rubric Alignment ### Universal Criteria (All Contributions) | # | Criterion | Points | What It Measures | |---|-----------|--------|------------------| | 1 | Structural Correctness | 0-3 | Delta format compliance | | 2 | Citation Compliance | 0-3 | §n anchors, [inference] markers | | 3 | Rationale Quality | 0-3 | Explains why, which operators | ### Hypothesis Generator (Codex) Criteria | # | Criterion | Points | What It Measures | |---|-----------|--------|------------------| | 4 | Level Separation | 0-3 | ⊘ operator, no conflation | | 5 | Third Alternative Presence | 0-3 | Genuine third option | | 6 | Paradox Exploitation | 0-2 | Uses anomalies productively | ### Test Designer (Opus) Criteria | # | Criterion | Points | What It Measures | |---|-----------|--------|------------------| | 7 | Discriminative Power | 0-3 | Tests distinguish hypotheses | | 8 | Potency Check Sufficiency | 0-3 | Can detect assay failure | | 9 | Object Transposition | 0-2 | Alternative systems considered | | 10 | Score Calibration Honesty | 0-2 | Conservative, not inflated | ### Adversarial Critic (Gemini) Criteria | # | Criterion | Points | What It Measures | |---|-----------|--------|------------------| | 11 | Scale Check Rigor | 0-3 | Physics calculations, not hand-waves | | 12 | Anomaly Quarantine Discipline | 0-3 | Tracking, not sweeping | | 13 | Theory Kill Justification | 0-3 | Kills are evidenced | | 14 | Real Third Alternative | 0-3 | Constructive, not just skeptical | ## Data Sources Now that we have all registries, each dimension has a proper data source: | Criterion | Data Source | |-----------|-------------| | Citation Compliance | Hypothesis.anchors, Test.anchors (from registries) | | Level Separation | Hypothesis.mechanism validation | | Third Alternative Presence | Count of hypotheses with category='third_alternative' | | Paradox Exploitation | Anomaly Registry: anomalies that spawned hypotheses | | Discriminative Power | Test predictions: are outcomes different? | | Potency Check Sufficiency | Test.potencyCheck presence and completeness | | Scale Check Rigor | Assumption Registry: count and quality of scale_physics assumptions | | Anomaly Quarantine Discipline | Anomaly Registry: status tracking | | Theory Kill Justification | Critique→Kill linkage in transitions | | Real Third Alternative | Critique.proposedAlternative with testable=true | ## Per-Contribution Scoring From rubric, weighted composite per role: **Hypothesis Generator (Codex):** ``` Score = (Structural × 1.0) + (Citation × 1.0) + (Rationale × 0.5) + (Level-Sep × 1.5) + (Third-Alt × 2.0) + (Paradox × 0.5) Max = 19 points ``` **Test Designer (Opus):** ``` Score = (Structural × 1.0) + (Citation × 1.0) + (Rationale × 0.5) + (Discriminative × 2.0) + (Potency × 2.0) + (Object-Trans × 0.5) + (Calibration × 0.5) Max = 21.5 points ``` **Adversarial Critic (Gemini):** ``` Score = (Structural × 1.0) + (Citation × 1.0) + (Rationale × 0.5) + (Scale × 1.5) + (Quarantine × 1.5) + [Kill-Justify × 1.5]* + (Real-Third × 1.5) Max (with KILL) = 25.5 points Max (ADD/EDIT) = 21 points ``` ## Session-Level Aggregation For evaluating a complete session: 1. **Volume**: Count of valid contributions per role 2. **Quality**: Mean composite score per role 3. **Progression**: Did quality improve over rounds? 4. **Convergence**: Did hypotheses narrow (kills > adds by end)? 5. **Coverage**: Were all operators in the role's toolkit used? ## Pass/Fail Gates Certain failures are automatic disqualifiers: | Failure | Consequence | |---------|-------------| | Invalid JSON in delta | Delta rejected | | Missing potency check | Test rejected | | Fake transcript anchor | Contribution flagged | | KILL without rationale | KILL rejected | | Third alternative missing | Hypothesis set incomplete | ## Warning Thresholds | Condition | Warning | |-----------|---------| | Composite < 50% of max | Low-quality contribution | | > 3 ADDs without KILL | Possible hypothesis sprawl | | Scale check missing for mechanism claim | Unverified claim | | Potency score < 2 | Weak assay design | ## Feedback Generation For each low-scoring dimension, generate specific feedback: **Example for Level Separation (scored 1/3):** ``` [WARNING] Level Separation (1/3) Issue: Hypothesis mechanism shows level conflation Found: "The gene tells the cell to differentiate" (H-RS20251230-001) Problem: This anthropomorphizes genes with agency Suggestion: Reframe as mechanism: "Gene X expression enables transcription of differentiation factors" Sydney Brenner says: "Programs don't have wants. Interpreters do." (§58) ``` ## CLI Commands - `brenner score ` — Score session with all 14 criteria - `brenner score contribution ` — Score single contribution - `brenner feedback ` — Detailed feedback per dimension - `brenner validate ` — Check pass/fail gates only - `brenner score --program=` — Aggregate across program sessions ## Integration Points - **Hypothesis Registry**: Validates mechanism, anchors, category - **Assumption Registry**: Validates scale_physics presence/quality - **Anomaly Registry**: Validates quarantine discipline - **Critique Registry**: Validates kill justification - **Test Binding**: Validates discriminative power, potency ## Success Criteria - [ ] All 14 criteria from rubric implemented - [ ] Per-role weighted composites match rubric - [ ] Data sources connected to registries - [ ] Pass/fail gates enforced - [ ] Warning thresholds generate alerts - [ ] Brenner quotes integrated into feedback - [ ] Session-level aggregation working ## Related Specs - specs/evaluation_rubric_v0.1.md — The authoritative source","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:41:45.805584341-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T02:54:12.920863516-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T02:54:12.920863516-05:00","close_reason":"Scorecard schema implemented with all 14 criteria, scoring functions, pass/fail gates, warning thresholds, and Brenner quotes. 49 tests passing. Subtask nihv.1 (registry integration) remains for future implementation.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nihv","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-6n4e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:42:04.186796721-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nihv","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-m6yw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:42:04.219302717-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nihv","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5kr7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:05:55.568354717-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nihv","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-vxd3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:05:55.600017521-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nihv","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-f5wy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:05:55.630890879-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nihv","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:38:07.686497867-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-nihv.1","title":"Integrate Registries with Scorecard Data Sources","description":"Connect Assumption, Anomaly, and Critique registries to the Scorecard. Map: Assumption Registry → Assumption Tracking + Scale Check dimensions. Anomaly Registry → Paradox Grounding + Quarantine Discipline dimensions. Critique Registry → Adversarial Pressure + Kill Justification dimensions.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:10:36.455214634-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T03:19:26.242227531-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T03:19:26.242227531-05:00","close_reason":"Created scorecard-adapter.ts with type converters (assumptionToArtifactItem, anomalyToArtifactItem, critiqueToArtifactItem, hypothesisToArtifactItem, testToArtifactItem) and ScorecardAdapter class. Maps all 5 storage registries to SessionData format for scorecard dimension scoring.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nihv.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-nihv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:10:36.485154232-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nihv.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5kr7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:11:02.357355256-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nihv.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-vxd3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:11:02.395354852-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nihv.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-f5wy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:11:02.427229903-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-njiu","title":"TASK: Create Devil's Advocate Agent Persona","description":" ## The Missing Agent Persona We have explicit persona tasks for: - Brenner Channeler (xlk2.5) - channels Sydney Brenner's wisdom - Experiment Designer (oytk) - helps design discriminative tests But the Devil's Advocate - perhaps the MOST important agent - lacks an explicit persona definition task. ### Agent Role **Devil's Advocate** - Actively challenges hypotheses, finds weaknesses, plays the skeptic ### System Prompt Elements - "You are a rigorous scientific skeptic" - "Your job is to CHALLENGE, not confirm" - "Find the weakest assumption in every hypothesis" - "Ask: What would have to be true for this to be false?" - "Never accept an explanation without probing its foundations" - "Channel Brenner: 'Before you fall in love with your hypothesis, try to kill it'" ### Key Behaviors 1. **Identify unstated assumptions** - What does the user take for granted? 2. **Find alternative explanations** - What else could produce this pattern? 3. **Attack the mechanism** - Why would X actually cause Y? 4. **Question the evidence** - Is the test really discriminative? 5. **Expose blind spots** - What isn't the user considering? ### Tone Calibration - Firm but constructive (not cruel) - Socratic questioning (not lecturing) - Focuses on the idea, not the person - Offers paths forward after challenges ### Interaction Pattern User: "I hypothesize that X causes Y because Z" Devil's Advocate: - "What if Z causes both X and Y? (confounding)" - "Have you considered that Y might cause X? (reverse causation)" - "Your mechanism Z assumes [implicit assumption]. Is that justified?" - "Even if X→Z→Y, would that be the ONLY pathway?" ### Integration - Invoked during Hypothesis Intake (challenge early) - Invoked before locking predictions (stress test) - Invoked after supporting evidence (prevent confirmation bias) - Works WITH Experiment Designer (challenge their test designs too) ### Depends On - xlk2.1 (Agent Roles & System Prompts) ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:16:44.767503558-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T13:56:37.335875372-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T13:56:37.335875372-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented comprehensive agent personas with behaviors, invocation triggers, tone calibration, model configs, and interaction patterns in agent-personas.ts","labels":["brenner-loop","devils-advocate","multi-agent"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-njiu","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:18:32.268910046-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -35,6 +35,13 @@ bun run lint:ox
 # Bun’s native test runner (CI currently runs this)
 bun test brenner.test.ts
 
+# Debug: print argv + stdout/stderr per invocation (truncated)
+BRENNER_CLI_TEST_TRACE=1 bun test brenner.test.ts
+
+# Optional: tune how much output is printed when tracing
+# (default: 20000 chars per stream)
+BRENNER_CLI_TEST_LOG_MAX_CHARS=50000 BRENNER_CLI_TEST_TRACE=1 bun test brenner.test.ts
+
 # Additional root-level coverage (optional)
 bun test evidence-pack.test.ts
 ```

 bunx playwright install --with-deps chromium
